item.title: Heyyyyyyyyyy….it’s me again
item.dc:creator: NegasonicAdultEMP

item.title: Most people say "someone else will stop and help" instead of "I'm going to stop and help"
item.description: Everything going into the bags for the homeless: Lotion and lip balm First aid kits Bottles of water and Gatorade Toothbrush and paste Shaving cream and razor Sunscreen Food/snacks Deodorant and shower wipes Hand sanitizer and female hygiene essentials Hand warmers 2 Packs of tissue 2 pairs Wool socks, 4 pairs socks, mittens, beanie Blanket Rain poncho Umbrella Narcan Pamphlets with information on shelter, food banks and recovery resources. And more! Everything was organized and put into a cinch
item.dc:creator: propagausi

item.title: Memes vol 346
item.description:
My boyfriend’s cat is named Bella but she’s really fat (we got her like that) so we call her Belly
And their smiles!!
We really did!
She’s living my dream
My dad named Timothy goes my Moth. No he doesn’t why’d I say that
I’m so bad with this. My boyfriend loves surprises so I bought us tickets for SuicideBoys and was immediately like “guess what we’re doing!”
Nessie tax
Liza Jane tax
Scruffy tax
Froggy tax

item.title: Public Education Deserves Funding
item.description: I’m grateful to live in Canada where I truly believe we have one of the best education systems in the world. However, where I live has had many years of right-leaning governments that has led to chronic underfunding of our public schools. Our current government is extremely right-wing and in its most recent funding announcement to build not-even-close-to-enough-schools, for the first time, has decided to start directing public funding to build private schools. This just further serves to undermine public education.
item.dc:creator: notarightienow

item.title: The pipe throwing puts this one over the top. +1 (sound on)
item.dc:creator: trustfallaccident

item.title: Forced Positivity
item.description:
1. Today the head gremlins of depression are being irritatingly loud.
2. This means it's time for obscene amounts of over the top positivity.
3. Years ago during my Divorce I started collecting positive things.
4. I would force myself to look at them when the head gremlins started to stir.
5. Sometimes it helped. Sometimes it didn't.
6. Honestly it was more useful to go through the process of finding the quotes than actually reading them.
7. I also kept a bunch of them like ammunition in my phone.
8. Say you're depressed on Facebook or Discord? Boom! Have a bunch of positivity!
9. It is oddly cathartic to bomb people with positivity when they are struggling. To become the person you needed in your own dark moments.
10. It is also a lot easier to look at other people's problems to try and fix them than actually dealing with being in your own head.
11. So here we are. Bombing Imgur with positivity to force myself to try and escape my own head.
12. This is my attempt to be productive while bed rotting.
13. It's preventing me from doing 'retail therapy' for those few seconds of happy chemicals when it shows up, only to then be stressed by figuring out where to actually store more clutter.
14. I haven't been active in my favorite hobby since Covid hit. I'm trying to force my brain to let me get back into things
15. I still use that hobby for the annoying "What makes you interesting?" questions people ask.
16. L.A.R.P. stands for Live Action Role Playing. I miss doing it so much.
17. I tell people about that because it makes me sound different. Interesting. Like I hold more value than I actually feel because I'm somehow memorable.
18. Because if I can bring more positivity into the world than negativity, I can somehow convince the head gremlins that I'm a good person.
19. That I deserve good things. That I need to stay here when the gremlins occasionally say to leave this world.
20. Over time it's amazing what you can get yourself used yourself to.
21. Trauma and depression become familiar. Like old friends that are terrible room mates.
22. A bully that lives in your head telling you the things that you would assure your friends are absolutely bullshit if someone else ever dared to say that to them.
23. Store bought happy chemicals help. Prescription numbness that takes the head gremlins from yelling to more of a dull roar.
24. Surviving every day is an absolute victory.
25. Losing so much in the past really does make you change your priorities in what you value versus what you're willing to let go of. Especially with people.
26. So today I'm forcing myself to value me. By doing the stupid little ritual I've developed of having pictures of sappy positivity.
27. And if you're reading this, I'm making it Your problem now. Because if I get to put up with this bullshit of trying to make my day better, then yours gets to be better too.
28. We're going through the motions together as basic bitches trying to Live, Laugh and Love. AKA Survive, Embrace Sarcasm and Dark Humor, and forcing ourselves into Self Acceptance.
29. I tell my current husband sometimes that we really need to get that Live, Laugh, Love picture frame with Skeletor in it.
30. More clutter to find the happy chemicals with. Silly things to make life a little brighter from day to day.
31. Last weekend I cleaned about a third of my room. Less clutter in my environment is helping with less brain clutter. Fighting tooth and nail to find the energy to do another third. Not going to say finish yet because that word is too over whelming. I didn't want to be self defeating.
32. Buying paper plates has helped the dishes to not overflow as badly. I'm better at taking out trash than washing dishes. Right now I'm trying to work around the things I can't overcome just yet. Accommodate my weaknesses instead of being defeated by them.
33. I even have a gym membership. It's still being more of a 'fat tax' with how rarely I use it, but ideally the cost of it will eventually guilt me into going more. I even got the fancy +1 membership to try and poke room mates for motivation in going. Holding each other accountable and what not.
34. I am trying to not beat myself up for being depressed. I know other people have worse issues and overall, I have enough stability in my life to survive. There is ramen in my cupboards and food in the cat dish. I need to be more thankful for that.
35. I've started being as positive as I can when I'm talking to other people to trick my brain into actually being more positive. Like training a muscle. It sucks and it is HARD. But I'm fighting through it.
36. I remember when my ex husband's told me I was like frosting in life. Nice and sweet, but not actually contributing to things over all in a meaningful way. Being chronically sick is a bitch, and he had to watch the process if me deteriorating. My health wouldn't actually let me be the equal partner we both wanted when we got married.
37. Hearing things like "If you existed in any other time period, you'd be in an insane asylum" messes with you. I get it now that I was basically a trauma for my ex to deal with. I don't blame him anymore for how he handled things. He was a secondary victim in the situation, and both of us were still young and stupid whole trying to figure life out together.
38. One of the most amazing things my current husband has ever said to me was "Anyone interesting in this day and age would have been in an asylum in the past. Hell, they would put women in there for Reading and thinking too much for themselves."
39. I've learned that my value as a person isn't Only defined by how much money I contribute to the household. That other contributions also can hold value as long as everyone communicates, works together, and agrees in how to divide the physical and emotional workload.
40. I'm trying to pay things forward. I had So much support after my divorce. Over the last few months we've gotten two room mates that I met online last year. Both of them moved here to escape abusive situations and move across the country to live with people they'd never met in person before.
41. In a week and a half a LARP friend is moving to our couch for the next six months. They were going to live in their car during the winter and have fibromyalgia. Talked to husband and the room mates to collectively decide this wasn't allowed to happen.
42. We're going to play Tetris to make everyones stuff fit here. The house is sometimes fondly referred to as a 'Millennial Hive'.
43. I'm anxious about having this many people in our space. That now there will be Two of us that are chronically ill. More people to buy groceries for. More people to share the bathroom. But I'm also really excited to have another LARPer in the house. Someone I can talk to in helping me to reclaim that part of myself again.
44. Every little thing helps in trying to sort my head. Tell off the head gremlins and find who I actually think of as who I am again. Not being this imposter every day that isn't living up to my Actual potential in life.
45. I'm finding myself more and more in helping people. Channeling enough forced positivity into the world to try and make my little corner of it a better place. Hiding my friends away in my extra rooms and on my couch who might be targeted as at risk by a Trumpsterfire. People with pronouns and flags.
46. I can't vacuum for myself right now. But maybe I can later today because I want to show my room mates and husband that they matter to me.
47. I want a cleaner space for my cats. I want less chores piled up that keep me from letting myself read books or catch up on Netflix in peace.
48. I want better for all of us. Myself and out in the world beyond.
49. This is why we circle back to all of this positive nonsense. Picture after picture of saccharine positivity to hopefully spark sometime better in people's heads.
50. Because Fuck You head gremlins! Not today!
item.dc:creator: GrayWolfieY2K

item.title: My family actually knew a guy in the 80s that had a perpetual pot of beans. It just stayed covered on low heat constantly.
item.description: He offered it to anyone who came over, but my parents would never let us try it. I had no idea it was a real thing done by other people. We always just thought he was crazy.
item.dc:creator: aModestProposalAndOtherRecipies

item.title: Walking today.
item.description: Walking today with ankle weights on both legs. It's took a few seconds to get my balance. But I was able to finish the walk in 1 minute. Really proud of my progress and that my walking speed has improved. I hope everyone is having a wonderful day.
item.dc:creator: kaosnole95

item.title: No, no, no, this is very bad
item.description:
The material being sprayed is liquid "foam" that hardens into a hydrophobic surface which will be the new roof. It has to be sprayed on a dry, solid surface, and has to adhere to said surface. It will definitely adhere to the shingles, however, the shingles are loose, and easily come off the plywood they're supposed to be nailed to. This will cause the foam to shift, crack, and become completely ineffective. The homeowner insisted we leave the shingles on, even after being told by the foreman, our supervisor, and her supervisor, still, dude wouldn't budge. This roof is going to last only a few months and will have to be torn off and redone. Thank you for letting me bore you, but I don't have friends to rant to, be well and stay fresh cheese bags.
Finished product, the empty space Is for the AC unit, we were quite upset, we pride ourselves in our work, and this was just crap.
item.dc:creator: Jaqdakloun
